steps.title: Attach the opening phrase
steps.content: The introductory phrase "Having 11,486 feet of prominence" must describe the noun that comes immediately after the comma. The thing with that prominence is Mount Hayes, not geographers, a list, or a ranking.

steps.title: Avoid dangling modifiers
steps.content: Choice A makes geographers the noun with prominence. Choice C makes a list have prominence. Choice D makes a ranking have prominence and also drops the needed article before "51st." The remaining version correctly puts Mount Hayes after the modifier. The answer is B.
